steps.title: Identify the initial population and target
steps.content: At $t = 0$, $f(0) = 60,000 \cdot 2^0 = 60,000$ bacteria. To double the population, we need $f(t) = 120,000$.

steps.title: Equate exponents and solve
steps.content: Since $2 = 2^1$, we have $2^{\frac{t}{410}} = 2^1$. Therefore, $\frac{t}{410} = 1$, which gives $t = 410$ minutes.
